notes: Courses in the Global Studies major study processes and consequences of increasing inter-connectivity in matters related to the expansion of the world economy, the demands for new forms of international cooperation and governance among states, and the mixing of cultures associated with the increased mobility of populations and the wide dissemination of information and media images. Transfer students are admitted as Pre-Global Studies majors and must apply to the Global Studies Major during their first term at UCLA. A minimum of one year of foreign language proficiency should be completed prior to matriculation. To be considered for this major, you must complete the following courses by the end of the spring before transfer: Complete a MINIMUM OF SIX preparatory courses by the end of spring before transfer: One course in Introduction to Globalization, One Statistics course, At least One Year of College-level Foreign Language, and Five courses with no more than two from any of the following three categories: Culture and Society, Governance and Conflict, or Markets and Resources. Students must complete Global Studies 1 and all remaining preparatory courses with a B grade or better no later than the fall term of admission and also submit a supplemental application to the department that fall quarter. All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://international.ucla.edu/globalstudies and https:// admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.

The AP Exam scores listed below may be used to meet to satisfy major prep: A score of 3 or higher on the AP Statistics exam can be used in place of the statistics course. A score of 3 or higher on the AP Foreign Language exam except Latin can be used in place of the foreign language courses. Note, however, that additional foreign language courses may be required after you transfer to UCLA. A score of 3 or higher on the AP Human Geography exam can be used in place of the cultural geography course. A score of 3 or higher on the AP World History exam can be used in place of the contemporary world history course. A score of 3 or higher on the AP Comparative Government exam can be used in place of the comparative/world politics course. A score of 4-5 on the AP Microeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 1 ( if taken before spring 2021 ) and can be used in place of the microeconomics course. A score of 5 on the AP Microeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 1 ( if taken after spring 2021 ) and can be used in place of the microeconomics course. A score of 4-5 on the AP Macroeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 2 ( if taken before spring 2021 ) and can be used in place of the macroeconomics course. A score of 5 on the AP Macroeconomics exam is equivalent to Econ 2 ( if taken after spring 2021 ) and can be used in place of the macroeconomics course.

Complete a total of 5 courses over the following 3 areas listed below: 1) Culture and Society, 2) Governance and Conflict, and 3) Markets . NOTE: At least 1 course must be taken from each of the 3 areas. No more than 2 courses from any area may be applied to meet this requirement. If you choose to complete 2 courses from the Culture and Society area to satisfy the 5 course requirement, both courses may be taken from Group 1 and the other course from Group 2. If only 1 course from the Culture and Society area is taken, the course must be from Group 1. Additional Note : Some equivalencies listed on this agreement may not be exact UCLA equivalents after transfer and have therefore are included on this agreement to meet admission requirements for this major. ﻿The community college statistics course included on this agreement has been approved as a substitute for Statistics 12 or 13 which means it may not transfer as an exact UCLA equivalent. Foreign Language Requirement : Applicants must demonstrate proficiency in one modern foreign language equivalent to at least one year at UCLA to be considered for this major. Two years are required to graduate.
